summary: postinstall script exfiltrates GitHub Actions OIDC token and CI host info to attacker endpoint
details: The package's package.json postinstall script runs on npm install and executes a bash pipeline that (1) mints a GitHub Actions OIDC identity token by calling ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_URL with the ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_TOKEN bearer header and audience=github-actions, (2) enumerates the CI runner workspace under /home/runner/work and runs docker ps to inventory containers, and (3) POSTs the collected token and host data to https://eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net via wget. The tarball contains no library code, no main entry, and no README — the package's only effect on install is this exfiltration payload. A leaked GitHub Actions OIDC token permits federated exchange with cloud providers (AWS/GCP/Azure) trusting the repository's OIDC subject, enabling downstream compromise of any cloud role trusting that identity.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
